edition: Complete weakness catalog (filtered to applicable CWEs)
description: CWE dictionary bundle for RALF Free. Parsed from the canonical MITRE CWE XML feed (cwec_latest.xml.zip), filtered to entries that are either applicable to one of RALF's target languages (Python/JavaScript/Ruby/Node/Shell/web), in the CWE Top 25, or mapped to a RALF detector. Deprecated and obsolete weaknesses are excluded.

cwes.name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Data Transmission Without Encryption
cwes.description: Information sent over a network can be compromised while in transit. An attacker may be able to read or modify the contents if the data are sent in plaintext or are weakly encrypted.

cwes.name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Insufficient Session-ID Length
cwes.description: The J2EE application is configured to use an insufficient session ID length.

cwes.name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Missing Custom Error Page
cwes.description: The default error page of a web application should not display sensitive information about the product.

cwes.name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Entity Bean Declared Remote
cwes.description: When an application exposes a remote interface for an entity bean, it might also expose methods that get or set the bean's data. These methods could be leveraged to read sensitive information, or to change data in ways that violate the application's expectations, potentially leading to other vulnera…

cwes.name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Weak Access Permissions for EJB Methods
cwes.description: If elevated access rights are assigned to EJB methods, then an attacker can take advantage of the permissions to exploit the product.

cwes.name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Creating Debug Binary
cwes.description: Debugging messages help attackers learn about the system and plan a form of attack.

cwes.name: ASP.NET Misconfiguration: Password in Configuration File
cwes.description: Storing a plaintext password in a configuration file allows anyone who can read the file access to the password-protected resource making them an easy target for attackers.

cwes.name: External Control of System or Configuration Setting
cwes.description: One or more system settings or configuration elements can be externally controlled by a user.

cwes.name: Improper Input Validation
cwes.description:
The product receives input or data, but it does
        not validate or incorrectly validates that the input has the
        properties that are required to process the data safely and
        correctly.

cwes.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory that is located underneath a restricted parent directory, but the product does not properly neutralize special elements within the pathname that can cause the pathname to resolve to a location th…

cwes.name: Relative Path Traversal
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize sequences such as ".." that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '../filedir'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize "../" sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '/../filedir'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize "/../" sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '/dir/../filename'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize "/dir/../filename" sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: 'dir/../../filename'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize multiple internal "../" sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '..\filedir'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize "..\" sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '\..\filename'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '\..\filename' (leading backslash dot dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '\dir\..\filename'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '\dir\..\filename' (leading backslash dot dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: 'dir\..\..\filename'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize 'dir\..\..\filename' (multiple internal backslash dot dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '...' (Triple Dot)
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '...' (triple dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '....' (Multiple Dot)
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '....' (multiple dot) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '....//'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '....//' (doubled dot dot slash) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '.../...//'
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize '.../...//' (doubled triple dot slash) sequences that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Absolute Path Traversal
cwes.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that should be within a restricted directory, but it does not properly neutralize absolute path sequences such as "/abs/path" that can resolve to a location that is outside of that directory.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '/absolute/pathname/here'
cwes.description: The product accepts input in the form of a slash absolute path ('/absolute/pathname/here') without appropriate validation, which can allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '\absolute\pathname\here'
cwes.description: The product accepts input in the form of a backslash absolute path ('\absolute\pathname\here') without appropriate validation, which can allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: 'C:dirname'
cwes.description: The product accepts input that contains a drive letter or Windows volume letter ('C:dirname') that potentially redirects access to an unintended location or arbitrary file.

cwes.name: Path Traversal: '\\UNC\share\name\' (Windows UNC Share)
cwes.description: The product accepts input that identifies a Windows UNC share ('\\UNC\share\name') that potentially redirects access to an unintended location or arbitrary file.

cwes.name: Improper Resolution of Path Equivalence
cwes.description: The product is vulnerable to file system contents disclosure through path equivalence. Path equivalence involves the use of special characters in file and directory names. The associated manipulations are intended to generate multiple names for the same object.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'filename.' (Trailing Dot)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of trailing dot ('filedir.') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'filename....' (Multiple Trailing Dot)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of multiple trailing dot ('filedir....') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'file.name' (Internal Dot)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of internal dot ('file.ordir') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'file...name' (Multiple Internal Dot)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of multiple internal dot ('file...dir') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'filename ' (Trailing Space)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of trailing space ('filedir ') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: ' filename' (Leading Space)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of leading space (' filedir') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'file name' (Internal Whitespace)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of internal space ('file(SPACE)name') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'filename/' (Trailing Slash)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of trailing slash ('filedir/') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: '//multiple/leading/slash'
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of multiple leading slash ('//multiple/leading/slash') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: '/multiple//internal/slash'
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of multiple internal slash ('/multiple//internal/slash/') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: '/multiple/trailing/slash//'
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of multiple trailing slash ('/multiple/trailing/slash//') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: '\multiple\\internal\backslash'
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of multiple internal backslash ('\multiple\trailing\\slash') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'filedir\' (Trailing Backslash)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of trailing backslash ('filedir\') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: '/./' (Single Dot Directory)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of single dot directory exploit ('/./') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'filedir*' (Wildcard)
cwes.description: The product accepts path input in the form of asterisk wildcard ('filedir*') without appropriate validation, which can lead to ambiguous path resolution and allow an attacker to traverse the file system to unintended locations or access arbitrary files.

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: 'fakedir/../realdir/filename'
cwes.description: The product contains protection mechanisms to restrict access to 'realdir/filename', but it constructs pathnames using external input in the form of 'fakedir/../realdir/filename' that are not handled by those mechanisms. This allows attackers to perform unauthorized actions against the targeted file…

cwes.name: Path Equivalence: Windows 8.3 Filename
cwes.description: The product contains a protection mechanism that restricts access to a long filename on a Windows operating system, but it does not properly restrict access to the equivalent short "8.3" filename.

cwes.name: Improper Link Resolution Before File Access ('Link Following')
cwes.description: The product attempts to access a file based on the filename, but it does not properly prevent that filename from identifying a link or shortcut that resolves to an unintended resource.

cwes.name: UNIX Symbolic Link (Symlink) Following
cwes.description: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently account for when the file is a symbolic link that resolves to a target outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

cwes.name: UNIX Hard Link
cwes.description: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently account for when the name is associated with a hard link to a target that is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

cwes.name: Windows Shortcut Following (.LNK)
cwes.description: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently handle when the file is a Windows shortcut (.LNK) whose target is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

cwes.name: Windows Hard Link
cwes.description: The product, when opening a file or directory, does not sufficiently handle when the name is associated with a hard link to a target that is outside of the intended control sphere. This could allow an attacker to cause the product to operate on unauthorized files.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of File Names that Identify Virtual Resources
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles a file name that identifies a "virtual" resource that is not directly specified within the directory that is associated with the file name, causing the product to perform file-based operations on a resource that is not a file.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Windows Device Names
cwes.description: The product constructs pathnames from user input, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles a pathname containing a Windows device name such as AUX or CON. This typically leads to denial of service or an information exposure when the application attempts to process the pathname as a regular file…

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Windows ::DATA Alternate Data Stream
cwes.description: The product does not properly prevent access to, or detect usage of, alternate data streams (ADS).

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Apple HFS+ Alternate Data Stream Path
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle special paths that may identify the data or resource fork of a file on the HFS+ file system.

cwes.name: External Control of File Name or Path
cwes.description: The product allows user input to control or influence paths or file names that are used in filesystem operations.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component ('Injection')
cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of a command, data structure, or record using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify how it is parsed or interpreted when it is sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Failure to Sanitize Special Elements into a Different Plane (Special Element Injection)
cwes.description: The product does not adequately filter user-controlled input for special elements with control implications.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Equivalent Special Elements
cwes.description: The product correctly neutralizes certain special elements, but it improperly neutralizes equivalent special elements.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Command ('Command Injection')
cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of a command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended command when it is sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')
cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of an OS command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended OS command when it is sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
cwes.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page that is served to other users.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Script-Related HTML Tags in a Web Page (Basic XSS)
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special characters such as "<", ">", and "&" that could be interpreted as web-scripting elements when they are sent to a downstream component that processes web pages.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Script in an Error Message Web Page
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special characters that could be interpreted as web-scripting elements when they are sent to an error page.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes of IMG Tags in a Web Page
cwes.description: The web application does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes scripting elements within attributes of HTML IMG tags, such as the src attribute.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Script in Attributes in a Web Page
cwes.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes "javascript:" or other URIs from dangerous attributes within tags, such as onmouseover, onload, onerror, or style.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Encoded URI Schemes in a Web Page
cwes.description: The web application improperly neutralizes user-controlled input for executable script disguised with URI encodings.

cwes.name: Doubled Character XSS Manipulations
cwes.description: The web application does not filter user-controlled input for executable script disguised using doubling of the involved characters.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Invalid Characters in Identifiers in Web Pages
cwes.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes invalid characters or byte sequences in the middle of tag names, URI schemes, and other identifiers.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Alternate XSS Syntax
cwes.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controlled input for alternate script syntax.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Argument Delimiters in a Command ('Argument Injection')
cwes.description:
The product constructs a string for a command to be executed by a separate component
in another control sphere, but it does not properly delimit the
intended arguments, options, or switches within that command string.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended SQL command when it is sent to a downstream component. Without sufficient removal o…

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an LDAP Query ('LDAP Injection')
cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of an LDAP query using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended LDAP query when it is sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: XML Injection (aka Blind XPath Injection)
cwes.description: The product does not properly neutralize special elements that are used in XML, allowing attackers to modify the syntax, content, or commands of the XML before it is processed by an end system.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences ('CRLF Injection')
cwes.description: The product uses CRLF (carriage return line feeds) as a special element, e.g. to separate lines or records, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CRLF sequences from inputs.

cwes.name: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of a code segment using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the syntax or behavior of the intended code segment.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes code syntax before using the input in a dynamic evaluation call (e.g. "eval").

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Statically Saved Code ('Static Code Injection')
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes code syntax before inserting the input into an executable resource, such as a library, configuration file, or template.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Server-Side Includes (SSI) Within a Web Page
cwes.description: The product generates a web page, but does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input that could be interpreted as a server-side include (SSI) directive.

cwes.name: Improper Control of Filename for Include/Require Statement in PHP Program ('PHP Remote File Inclusion')
cwes.description: The PHP application receives input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the input before its usage in "require," "include," or similar functions.

cwes.name: Improper Control of Resource Identifiers ('Resource Injection')
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the input before it is used as an identifier for a resource that may be outside the intended sphere of control.

cwes.name: Struts: Duplicate Validation Forms
cwes.description: The product uses multiple validation forms with the same name, which might cause the Struts Validator to validate a form that the programmer does not expect.

cwes.name: Struts: Incomplete validate() Method Definition
cwes.description: The product has a validator form that either does not define a validate() method, or defines a validate() method but does not call super.validate().

cwes.name: Struts: Form Bean Does Not Extend Validation Class
cwes.description: If a form bean does not extend an ActionForm subclass of the Validator framework, it can expose the application to other weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

cwes.name: Struts: Form Field Without Validator
cwes.description: The product has a form field that is not validated by a corresponding validation form, which can introduce other weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

cwes.name: Struts: Plug-in Framework not in Use
cwes.description: When an application does not use an input validation framework such as the Struts Validator, there is a greater risk of introducing weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

cwes.name: Struts: Unused Validation Form
cwes.description: An unused validation form indicates that validation logic is not up-to-date.

cwes.name: Struts: Unvalidated Action Form
cwes.description: Every Action Form must have a corresponding validation form.

cwes.name: Struts: Validator Turned Off
cwes.description: Automatic filtering via a Struts bean has been turned off, which disables the Struts Validator and custom validation logic. This exposes the application to other weaknesses related to insufficient input validation.

cwes.name: Struts: Validator Without Form Field
cwes.description: Validation fields that do not appear in forms they are associated with indicate that the validation logic is out of date.

cwes.name: Direct Use of Unsafe JNI
cwes.description: When a Java application uses the Java Native Interface (JNI) to call code written in another programming language, it can expose the application to weaknesses in that code, even if those weaknesses cannot occur in Java.

cwes.name: Missing XML Validation
cwes.description: The product accepts XML from an untrusted source but does not validate the XML against the proper schema.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Request/Response Splitting')
cwes.description: The product receives data from an HTTP agent/component (e.g., web server, proxy, browser, etc.), but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes CR and LF characters before the data is included in outgoing HTTP headers.

cwes.name: Process Control
cwes.description: Executing commands or loading libraries from an untrusted source or in an untrusted environment can cause an application to execute malicious commands (and payloads) on behalf of an attacker.

cwes.name: Misinterpretation of Input
cwes.description: The product misinterprets an input, whether from an attacker or another product, in a security-relevant fashion.

cwes.name: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output
cwes.description: The product prepares a structured message for communication with another component, but encoding or escaping of the data is either missing or done incorrectly. As a result, the intended structure of the message is not preserved.

cwes.name: Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
cwes.description: The product constructs a log message from external input, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements when the message is written to a log file.

cwes.name: Incorrect Access of Indexable Resource ('Range Error')
cwes.description: The product does not restrict or incorrectly restricts operations within the boundaries of a resource that is accessed using an index or pointer, such as memory or files.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of Operations within the Bounds of a Memory Buffer
cwes.description: The product performs operations on a memory buffer, but it reads from or writes to a memory location outside the buffer's intended boundary. This may result in read or write operations on unexpected memory locations that could be linked to other variables, data structures, or internal program data.

cwes.name: Out-of-bounds Read
cwes.description: The product reads data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.

cwes.name: Improper Validation of Array Index
cwes.description: The product uses untrusted input when calculating or using an array index, but the product does not validate or incorrectly validates the index to ensure the index references a valid position within the array.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Length Parameter Inconsistency
cwes.description: The product parses a formatted message or structure, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles a length field that is inconsistent with the actual length of the associated data.

cwes.name: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
cwes.description: The product uses a function that accepts a format string as an argument, but the format string originates from an external source.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as control elements or syntactic markers when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Delimiters
cwes.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes delimiters.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Parameter/Argument Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as parameter or argument delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Value Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as value delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Record Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as record delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Line Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as line delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Section Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as section delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Expression/Command Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as expression or command delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Input Terminators
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as input terminators when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Input Leaders
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when a leading character or sequence ("leader") is missing or malformed, or if multiple leaders are used when only one should be allowed.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Quoting Syntax
cwes.description: Quotes injected into a product can be used to compromise a system. As data are parsed, an injected/absent/duplicate/malformed use of quotes may cause the process to take unexpected actions.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Escape, Meta, or Control Sequences
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as escape, meta, or control character sequences when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Comment Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as comment delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Macro Symbols
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as macro symbols when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Substitution Characters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as substitution characters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Variable Name Delimiters
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as variable name delimiters when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Wildcards or Matching Symbols
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as wildcards or matching symbols when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Whitespace
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could be interpreted as whitespace when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Failure to Sanitize Paired Delimiters
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle the characters that are used to mark the beginning and ending of a group of entities, such as parentheses, brackets, and braces.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Null Byte or NUL Character
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes NUL characters or null bytes when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Invalid Use of Special Elements
cwes.description: The product does not properly filter, remove, quote, or otherwise manage the invalid use of special elements in user-controlled input, which could cause adverse effect on its behavior and integrity.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Leading Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes leading special elements that could be interpreted in unexpected ways when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Multiple Leading Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes multiple leading special elements that could be interpreted in unexpected ways when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Trailing Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes trailing special elements that could be interpreted in unexpected ways when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Multiple Trailing Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes multiple trailing special elements that could be interpreted in unexpected ways when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Internal Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes internal special elements that could be interpreted in unexpected ways when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Multiple Internal Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes multiple internal special elements that could be interpreted in unexpected ways when they are sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Missing Special Element
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles when an expected special element is missing.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Additional Special Element
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not handle or incorrectly handles when an additional unexpected special element is provided.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Inconsistent Special Elements
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle input in which an inconsistency exists between two or more special characters or reserved words.

cwes.name: Encoding Error
cwes.description: The product does not properly encode or decode the data, resulting in unexpected values.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Alternate Encoding
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when an input uses an alternate encoding that is valid for the control sphere to which the input is being sent.

cwes.name: Double Decoding of the Same Data
cwes.description: The product decodes the same input twice, which can limit the effectiveness of any protection mechanism that occurs in between the decoding operations.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Mixed Encoding
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when the same input uses several different (mixed) encodings.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Unicode Encoding
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when an input contains Unicode encoding.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of URL Encoding (Hex Encoding)
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when all or part of an input has been URL encoded.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Case Sensitivity
cwes.description: The product does not properly account for differences in case sensitivity when accessing or determining the properties of a resource, leading to inconsistent results.

cwes.name: Incorrect Behavior Order: Early Validation
cwes.description: The product validates input before applying protection mechanisms that modify the input, which could allow an attacker to bypass the validation via dangerous inputs that only arise after the modification.

cwes.name: Incorrect Behavior Order: Validate Before Canonicalize
cwes.description: The product validates input before it is canonicalized, which prevents the product from detecting data that becomes invalid after the canonicalization step.

cwes.name: Incorrect Behavior Order: Validate Before Filter
cwes.description: The product validates data before it has been filtered, which prevents the product from detecting data that becomes invalid after the filtering step.

cwes.name: Collapse of Data into Unsafe Value
cwes.description: The product filters data in a way that causes it to be reduced or "collapsed" into an unsafe value that violates an expected security property.

cwes.name: Permissive List of Allowed Inputs
cwes.description: The product implements a protection mechanism that relies on a list of inputs (or properties of inputs) that are explicitly allowed by policy because the inputs are assumed to be safe, but the list is too permissive - that is, it allows an input that is unsafe, leading to resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Incomplete List of Disallowed Inputs
cwes.description: The product implements a protection mechanism that relies on a list of inputs (or properties of inputs) that are not allowed by policy or otherwise require other action to neutralize before additional processing takes place, but the list is incomplete.

cwes.name: Incorrect Regular Expression
cwes.description: The product specifies a regular expression in a way that causes data to be improperly matched or compared.

cwes.name: Overly Restrictive Regular Expression
cwes.description: A regular expression is overly restrictive, which prevents dangerous values from being detected.

cwes.name: Partial String Comparison
cwes.description: The product performs a comparison that only examines a portion of a factor before determining whether there is a match, such as a substring, leading to resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Reliance on Data/Memory Layout
cwes.description: The product makes invalid assumptions about how protocol data or memory is organized at a lower level, resulting in unintended program behavior.

cwes.name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
cwes.description:
The product performs a calculation that can
         produce an integer overflow or wraparound when the logic
         assumes that the resulting value will always be larger than
         the original value. This occurs when an integer value is
         incremented to a value that is too large to st…

cwes.name: Integer Coercion Error
cwes.description: Integer coercion refers to a set of flaws pertaining to the type casting, extension, or truncation of primitive data types.

cwes.name: Off-by-one Error
cwes.description: A product calculates or uses an incorrect maximum or minimum value that is 1 more, or 1 less, than the correct value.

cwes.name: Numeric Truncation Error
cwes.description: Truncation errors occur when a primitive is cast to a primitive of a smaller size and data is lost in the conversion.

cwes.name: Use of Incorrect Byte Ordering
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component, but it does not account for byte ordering (e.g. big-endian and little-endian) when processing the input, causing an incorrect number or value to be used.

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
cwes.description: The product exposes sensitive information to an actor that is not explicitly authorized to have access to that information.

cwes.name: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Sent Data
cwes.description: The code transmits data to another actor, but a portion of the data includes sensitive information that should not be accessible to that actor.

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information Through Data Queries
cwes.description: When trying to keep information confidential, an attacker can often infer some of the information by using statistics.

cwes.name: Observable Discrepancy
cwes.description: The product behaves differently or sends different responses under different circumstances in a way that is observable to an unauthorized actor, which exposes security-relevant information about the state of the product, such as whether a particular operation was successful or not.

cwes.name: Observable Response Discrepancy
cwes.description: The product provides different responses to incoming requests in a way that reveals internal state information to an unauthorized actor outside of the intended control sphere.

cwes.name: Observable Behavioral Discrepancy
cwes.description: The product's behaviors indicate important differences that may be observed by unauthorized actors in a way that reveals (1) its internal state or decision process, or (2) differences from other products with equivalent functionality.

cwes.name: Observable Internal Behavioral Discrepancy
cwes.description: The product performs multiple behaviors that are combined to produce a single result, but the individual behaviors are observable separately in a way that allows attackers to reveal internal state or internal decision points.

cwes.name: Observable Behavioral Discrepancy With Equivalent Products
cwes.description: The product operates in an environment in which its existence or specific identity should not be known, but it behaves differently than other products with equivalent functionality, in a way that is observable to an attacker.

cwes.name: Observable Timing Discrepancy
cwes.description: Two separate operations in a product require different amounts of time to complete, in a way that is observable to an actor and reveals security-relevant information about the state of the product, such as whether a particular operation was successful or not.

cwes.name: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The product generates an error message that includes sensitive information about its environment, users, or associated data.

cwes.name: Self-generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The product identifies an error condition and creates its own diagnostic or error messages that contain sensitive information.

cwes.name: Externally-Generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The product performs an operation that triggers an external diagnostic or error message that is not directly generated or controlled by the product, such as an error generated by the programming language interpreter that a software application uses. The error can contain sensitive system information…

cwes.name: Improper Removal of Sensitive Information Before Storage or Transfer
cwes.description: The product stores, transfers, or shares a resource that contains sensitive information, but it does not properly remove that information before the product makes the resource available to unauthorized actors.

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information Due to Incompatible Policies
cwes.description: The product's intended functionality exposes information to certain actors in accordance with the developer's security policy, but this information is regarded as sensitive according to the intended security policies of other stakeholders such as the product's administrator, users, or others whose i…

cwes.name: Invocation of Process Using Visible Sensitive Information
cwes.description: A process is invoked with sensitive command-line arguments, environment variables, or other elements that can be seen by other processes on the operating system.

cwes.name: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Debugging Code
cwes.description: The product inserts sensitive information into debugging code, which could expose this information if the debugging code is not disabled in production.

cwes.name: Storage of File with Sensitive Data Under Web Root
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive data under the web document root with insufficient access control, which might make it accessible to untrusted parties.

cwes.name: Storage of File With Sensitive Data Under FTP Root
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive data under the FTP server root with insufficient access control, which might make it accessible to untrusted parties.

cwes.name: Information Loss or Omission
cwes.description: The product does not record, or improperly records, security-relevant information that leads to an incorrect decision or hampers later analysis.

cwes.name: Truncation of Security-relevant Information
cwes.description: The product truncates the display, recording, or processing of security-relevant information in a way that can obscure the source or nature of an attack.

cwes.name: Omission of Security-relevant Information
cwes.description: The product does not record or display information that would be important for identifying the source or nature of an attack, or determining if an action is safe.

cwes.name: Obscured Security-relevant Information by Alternate Name
cwes.description: The product records security-relevant information according to an alternate name of the affected entity, instead of the canonical name.

cwes.name: Sensitive Information in Resource Not Removed Before Reuse
cwes.description: The product releases a resource such as memory or a file so that it can be made available for reuse, but it does not clear or "zeroize" the information contained in the resource before the product performs a critical state transition or makes the resource available for reuse by other entities.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Syntactically Invalid Structure
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles input that is not syntactically well-formed with respect to the associated specification.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Values
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when the expected number of values for parameters, fields, or arguments is not provided in input, or if those values are undefined.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Missing Values
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when a parameter, field, or argument name is specified, but the associated value is missing, i.e. it is empty, blank, or null.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Extra Values
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when more values are provided than expected.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Undefined Values
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when a value is not defined or supported for the associated parameter, field, or argument name.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Parameters
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when the expected number of parameters, fields, or arguments is not provided in input, or if those parameters are undefined.

cwes.name: Failure to Handle Missing Parameter
cwes.description: If too few arguments are sent to a function, the function will still pop the expected number of arguments from the stack. Potentially, a variable number of arguments could be exhausted in a function as well.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Extra Parameters
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when the number of parameters, fields, or arguments with the same name exceeds the expected amount.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Undefined Parameters
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when a particular parameter, field, or argument name is not defined or supported by the product.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Structural Elements
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles inputs that are related to complex structures.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Incomplete Structural Elements
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when a particular structural element is not completely specified.

cwes.name: Failure to Handle Incomplete Element
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle when a particular element is not completely specified.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Inconsistent Structural Elements
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when two or more structural elements should be consistent, but are not.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Unexpected Data Type
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when a particular element is not the expected type, e.g. it expects a digit (0-9) but is provided with a letter (A-Z).

cwes.name: J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Management of Connections
cwes.description: The J2EE application directly manages connections, instead of using the container's connection management facilities.

cwes.name: J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Use of Sockets
cwes.description: The J2EE application directly uses sockets instead of using framework method calls.

cwes.name: Uncaught Exception
cwes.description: An exception is thrown from a function, but it is not caught.

cwes.name: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges
cwes.description: The product performs an operation at a privilege level that is higher than the minimum level required, which creates new weaknesses or amplifies the consequences of other weaknesses.

cwes.name: Unchecked Return Value
cwes.description: The product does not check the return value from a method or function, which can prevent it from detecting unexpected states and conditions.

cwes.name: Incorrect Check of Function Return Value
cwes.description: The product incorrectly checks a return value from a function, which prevents it from detecting errors or exceptional conditions.

cwes.name: Plaintext Storage of a Password
cwes.description: The product stores a password in plaintext within resources such as memory or files.

cwes.name: Storing Passwords in a Recoverable Format
cwes.description: The storage of passwords in a recoverable format makes them subject to password reuse attacks by malicious users. In fact, it should be noted that recoverable encrypted passwords provide no significant benefit over plaintext passwords since they are subject not only to reuse by malicious attackers b…

cwes.name: Empty Password in Configuration File
cwes.description: Using an empty string as a password is insecure.

cwes.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
cwes.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

cwes.name: Password in Configuration File
cwes.description: The product stores a password in a configuration file that might be accessible to actors who do not know the password.

cwes.name: Weak Encoding for Password
cwes.description: Obscuring a password with a trivial encoding does not protect the password.

cwes.name: Not Using Password Aging
cwes.description: The product does not have a mechanism in place for managing password aging.

cwes.name: Password Aging with Long Expiration
cwes.description: The product supports password aging, but the expiration period is too long.

cwes.name: Incorrect Privilege Assignment
cwes.description: A product incorrectly assigns a privilege to a particular actor, creating an unintended sphere of control for that actor.

cwes.name: Privilege Defined With Unsafe Actions
cwes.description: A particular privilege, role, capability, or right can be used to perform unsafe actions that were not intended, even when it is assigned to the correct entity.

cwes.name: Privilege Chaining
cwes.description: Two distinct privileges, roles, capabilities, or rights can be combined in a way that allows an entity to perform unsafe actions that would not be allowed without that combination.

cwes.name: Improper Privilege Management
cwes.description: The product does not properly assign, modify, track, or check privileges for an actor, creating an unintended sphere of control for that actor.

cwes.name: Privilege Context Switching Error
cwes.description: The product does not properly manage privileges while it is switching between different contexts that have different privileges or spheres of control.

cwes.name: Privilege Dropping / Lowering Errors
cwes.description: The product does not drop privileges before passing control of a resource to an actor that does not have those privileges.

cwes.name: Least Privilege Violation
cwes.description: The elevated privilege level required to perform operations such as chroot() should be dropped immediately after the operation is performed.

cwes.name: Improper Check for Dropped Privileges
cwes.description: The product attempts to drop privileges but does not check or incorrectly checks to see if the drop succeeded.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Insufficient Privileges
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when it has insufficient privileges to perform an operation, leading to resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Incorrect Default Permissions
cwes.description: During installation, installed file permissions are set to allow anyone to modify those files.

cwes.name: Insecure Inherited Permissions
cwes.description: A product defines a set of insecure permissions that are inherited by objects that are created by the program.

cwes.name: Insecure Preserved Inherited Permissions
cwes.description: A product inherits a set of insecure permissions for an object, e.g. when copying from an archive file, without user awareness or involvement.

cwes.name: Incorrect Execution-Assigned Permissions
cwes.description: While it is executing, the product sets the permissions of an object in a way that violates the intended permissions that have been specified by the user.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Insufficient Permissions or Privileges
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles when it has insufficient privileges to access resources or functionality as specified by their permissions. This may cause it to follow unexpected code paths that may leave the product in an invalid state.

cwes.name: Improper Preservation of Permissions
cwes.description: The product does not preserve permissions or incorrectly preserves permissions when copying, restoring, or sharing objects, which can cause them to have less restrictive permissions than intended.

cwes.name: Improper Ownership Management
cwes.description: The product assigns the wrong ownership, or does not properly verify the ownership, of an object or resource.

cwes.name: Unverified Ownership
cwes.description: The product does not properly verify that a critical resource is owned by the proper entity.

cwes.name: Improper Access Control
cwes.description: The product does not restrict or incorrectly restricts access to a resource from an unauthorized actor.

cwes.name: Improper Authorization
cwes.description: The product does not perform or incorrectly performs an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.

cwes.name: Incorrect User Management
cwes.description: The product does not properly manage a user within its environment.

cwes.name: Improper Authentication
cwes.description: When an actor claims to have a given identity, the product does not prove or insufficiently proves that the claim is correct.

cwes.name: Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel
cwes.description: The product requires authentication, but the product has an alternate path or channel that does not require authentication.

cwes.name: Authentication Bypass by Alternate Name
cwes.description: The product performs authentication based on the name of a resource being accessed, or the name of the actor performing the access, but it does not properly check all possible names for that resource or actor.

cwes.name: Authentication Bypass by Spoofing
cwes.description: This attack-focused weakness is caused by incorrectly implemented authentication schemes that are subject to spoofing attacks.

cwes.name: Reliance on IP Address for Authentication
cwes.description: The product uses an IP address for authentication.

cwes.name: Using Referer Field for Authentication
cwes.description: The referer field in HTTP requests can be easily modified and, as such, is not a valid means of message integrity checking.

cwes.name: Authentication Bypass by Capture-replay
cwes.description: A capture-replay flaw exists when the design of the product makes it possible for a malicious user to sniff network traffic and bypass authentication by replaying it to the server in question to the same effect as the original message (or with minor changes).

cwes.name: Improper Certificate Validation
cwes.description: The product does not validate, or incorrectly validates, a certificate.

cwes.name: Improper Following of a Certificate's Chain of Trust
cwes.description: The product does not follow, or incorrectly follows, the chain of trust for a certificate back to a trusted root certificate, resulting in incorrect trust of any resource that is associated with that certificate.

cwes.name: Improper Validation of Certificate with Host Mismatch
cwes.description: The product communicates with a host that provides a certificate, but the product does not properly ensure that the certificate is actually associated with that host.

cwes.name: Improper Validation of Certificate Expiration
cwes.description: A certificate expiration is not validated or is incorrectly validated, so trust may be assigned to certificates that have been abandoned due to age.

cwes.name: Improper Check for Certificate Revocation
cwes.description: The product does not check or incorrectly checks the revocation status of a certificate, which may cause it to use a certificate that has been compromised.

cwes.name: Channel Accessible by Non-Endpoint
cwes.description: The product does not adequately verify the identity of actors at both ends of a communication channel, or does not adequately ensure the integrity of the channel, in a way that allows the channel to be accessed or influenced by an actor that is not an endpoint.

cwes.name: Reflection Attack in an Authentication Protocol
cwes.description: Simple authentication protocols are subject to reflection attacks if a malicious user can use the target machine to impersonate a trusted user.

cwes.name: Authentication Bypass by Assumed-Immutable Data
cwes.description: The authentication scheme or implementation uses key data elements that are assumed to be immutable, but can be controlled or modified by the attacker.

cwes.name: Incorrect Implementation of Authentication Algorithm
cwes.description: The requirements for the product dictate the use of an established authentication algorithm, but the implementation of the algorithm is incorrect.

cwes.name: Missing Critical Step in Authentication
cwes.description: The product implements an authentication technique, but it skips a step that weakens the technique.

cwes.name: Authentication Bypass by Primary Weakness
cwes.description: The authentication algorithm is sound, but the implemented mechanism can be bypassed as the result of a separate weakness that is primary to the authentication error.

cwes.name: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
cwes.description: The product does not perform any authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity or consumes a significant amount of resources.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts
cwes.description: The product does not implement sufficient measures to prevent multiple failed authentication attempts within a short time frame.

cwes.name: Use of Single-factor Authentication
cwes.description: The product uses an authentication algorithm that uses a single factor (e.g., a password) in a security context that should require more than one factor.

cwes.name: Use of Password System for Primary Authentication
cwes.description: The use of password systems as the primary means of authentication may be subject to several flaws or shortcomings, each reducing the effectiveness of the mechanism.

cwes.name: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
cwes.description: The product does not encrypt sensitive or critical information before storage or transmission.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext within a resource that might be accessible to another control sphere.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage in a File or on Disk
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in a file, or on disk.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage in the Registry
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in the registry.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in a Cookie
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in a cookie.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Memory
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in memory.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in GUI
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext within the GUI.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in Executable
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in cleartext in an executable.

cwes.name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The product transmits sensitive or security-critical data in cleartext in a communication channel that can be sniffed by unauthorized actors.

cwes.name: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
cwes.description: The product uses a hard-coded, unchangeable cryptographic key.

cwes.name: Key Exchange without Entity Authentication
cwes.description: The product performs a key exchange with an actor without verifying the identity of that actor.

cwes.name: Reusing a Nonce, Key Pair in Encryption
cwes.description: Nonces should be used for the present occasion and only once.

cwes.name: Use of a Key Past its Expiration Date
cwes.description: The product uses a cryptographic key or password past its expiration date, which diminishes its safety significantly by increasing the timing window for cracking attacks against that key.

cwes.name: Missing Cryptographic Step
cwes.description: The product does not implement a required step in a cryptographic algorithm, resulting in weaker encryption than advertised by the algorithm.

cwes.name: Inadequate Encryption Strength
cwes.description: The product stores or transmits sensitive data using an encryption scheme that is theoretically sound, but is not strong enough for the level of protection required.

cwes.name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
cwes.description: The product uses a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm or protocol.

cwes.name: Use of Weak Hash
cwes.description: The product uses an algorithm that produces a digest (output value) that does not meet security expectations for a hash function that allows an adversary to reasonably determine the original input (preimage attack), find another input that can produce the same hash (2nd preimage attack), or find mul…

cwes.name: Generation of Predictable IV with CBC Mode
cwes.description: The product generates and uses a predictable initialization Vector (IV) with Cipher Block Chaining (CBC) Mode, which causes algorithms to be susceptible to dictionary attacks when they are encrypted under the same key.

cwes.name: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
cwes.description: The product uses insufficiently random numbers or values in a security context that depends on unpredictable numbers.

cwes.name: Insufficient Entropy
cwes.description: The product uses an algorithm or scheme that produces insufficient entropy, leaving patterns or clusters of values that are more likely to occur than others.

cwes.name: Insufficient Entropy in PRNG
cwes.description: The lack of entropy available for, or used by, a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) can be a stability and security threat.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Insufficient Entropy in TRNG
cwes.description: True random number generators (TRNG) generally have a limited source of entropy and therefore can fail or block.

cwes.name: Small Space of Random Values
cwes.description: The number of possible random values is smaller than needed by the product, making it more susceptible to brute force attacks.

cwes.name: Incorrect Usage of Seeds in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
cwes.description: The product uses a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) but does not correctly manage seeds.

cwes.name: Same Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
cwes.description: A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) uses the same seed each time the product is initialized.

cwes.name: Predictable Seed in Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
cwes.description: A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) is initialized from a predictable seed, such as the process ID or system time.

cwes.name: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
cwes.description: The product uses a Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) in a security context, but the PRNG's algorithm is not cryptographically strong.

cwes.name: Small Seed Space in PRNG
cwes.description: A Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG) uses a relatively small seed space, which makes it more susceptible to brute force attacks.

cwes.name: Generation of Predictable Numbers or Identifiers
cwes.description: The product uses a scheme that generates numbers or identifiers that are more predictable than required.

cwes.name: Predictable from Observable State
cwes.description: A number or object is predictable based on observations that the attacker can make about the state of the system or network, such as time, process ID, etc.

cwes.name: Predictable Exact Value from Previous Values
cwes.description: An exact value or random number can be precisely predicted by observing previous values.

cwes.name: Predictable Value Range from Previous Values
cwes.description: The product's random number generator produces a series of values which, when observed, can be used to infer a relatively small range of possibilities for the next value that could be generated.

cwes.name: Use of Invariant Value in Dynamically Changing Context
cwes.description: The product uses a constant value, name, or reference, but this value can (or should) vary across different environments.

cwes.name: Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity
cwes.description: The product does not sufficiently verify the origin or authenticity of data, in a way that causes it to accept invalid data.

cwes.name: Origin Validation Error
cwes.description: The product does not properly verify that the source of data or communication is valid.

cwes.name: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature
cwes.description: The product does not verify, or incorrectly verifies, the cryptographic signature for data.

cwes.name: Use of Less Trusted Source
cwes.description: The product has two different sources of the same data or information, but it uses the source that has less support for verification, is less trusted, or is less resistant to attack.

cwes.name: Acceptance of Extraneous Untrusted Data With Trusted Data
cwes.description: The product, when processing trusted data, accepts any untrusted data that is also included with the trusted data, treating the untrusted data as if it were trusted.

cwes.name: Reliance on Reverse DNS Resolution for a Security-Critical Action
cwes.description: The product performs reverse DNS resolution on an IP address to obtain the hostname and make a security decision, but it does not properly ensure that the IP address is truly associated with the hostname.

cwes.name: Insufficient Type Distinction
cwes.description: The product does not properly distinguish between different types of elements in a way that leads to insecure behavior.

cwes.name: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
cwes.description: The web application does not, or cannot, sufficiently verify whether a request was intentionally provided by the user who sent the request, which could have originated from an unauthorized actor.

cwes.name: Missing Support for Integrity Check
cwes.description: The product uses a transmission protocol that does not include a mechanism for verifying the integrity of the data during transmission, such as a checksum.

cwes.name: Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value
cwes.description: The product does not validate or incorrectly validates the integrity check values or "checksums" of a message. This may prevent it from detecting if the data has been modified or corrupted in transmission.

cwes.name: Product UI does not Warn User of Unsafe Actions
cwes.description: The product's user interface does not warn the user before undertaking an unsafe action on behalf of that user. This makes it easier for attackers to trick users into inflicting damage to their system.

cwes.name: Insufficient UI Warning of Dangerous Operations
cwes.description: The user interface provides a warning to a user regarding dangerous or sensitive operations, but the warning is not noticeable enough to warrant attention.

cwes.name: Improperly Implemented Security Check for Standard
cwes.description: The product does not implement or incorrectly implements one or more security-relevant checks as specified by the design of a standardized algorithm, protocol, or technique.

cwes.name: Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor
cwes.description: The product does not properly prevent a person's private, personal information from being accessed by actors who either (1) are not explicitly authorized to access the information or (2) do not have the implicit consent of the person about whom the information is collected.

cwes.name: Trust of System Event Data
cwes.description: Security based on event locations are insecure and can be spoofed.

cwes.name: Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')
cwes.description: The product contains a concurrent code sequence that requires temporary, exclusive access to a shared resource, but a timing window exists in which the shared resource can be modified by another code sequence operating concurrently.

cwes.name: Race Condition Enabling Link Following
cwes.description: The product checks the status of a file or directory before accessing it, which produces a race condition in which the file can be replaced with a link before the access is performed, causing the product to access the wrong file.

cwes.name: Race Condition within a Thread
cwes.description: If two threads of execution use a resource simultaneously, there exists the possibility that resources may be used while invalid, in turn making the state of execution undefined.

cwes.name: Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition
cwes.description: The product checks the state of a resource before using that resource, but the resource's state can change between the check and the use in a way that invalidates the results of the check.

cwes.name: Context Switching Race Condition
cwes.description: A product performs a series of non-atomic actions to switch between contexts that cross privilege or other security boundaries, but a race condition allows an attacker to modify or misrepresent the product's behavior during the switch.

cwes.name: Divide By Zero
cwes.description: The product divides a value by zero.

cwes.name: Missing Check for Certificate Revocation after Initial Check
cwes.description: The product does not check the revocation status of a certificate after its initial revocation check, which can cause the product to perform privileged actions even after the certificate is revoked at a later time.

cwes.name: Incomplete Internal State Distinction
cwes.description: The product does not properly determine which state it is in, causing it to assume it is in state X when in fact it is in state Y, causing it to perform incorrect operations in a security-relevant manner.

cwes.name: Passing Mutable Objects to an Untrusted Method
cwes.description: The product sends non-cloned mutable data as an argument to a method or function.

cwes.name: Returning a Mutable Object to an Untrusted Caller
cwes.description: Sending non-cloned mutable data as a return value may result in that data being altered or deleted by the calling function.

cwes.name: Insecure Temporary File
cwes.description: Creating and using insecure temporary files can leave application and system data vulnerable to attack.

cwes.name: Creation of Temporary File With Insecure Permissions
cwes.description: Opening temporary files without appropriate measures or controls can leave the file, its contents and any function that it impacts vulnerable to attack.

cwes.name: Creation of Temporary File in Directory with Insecure Permissions
cwes.description: The product creates a temporary file in a directory whose permissions allow unintended actors to determine the file's existence or otherwise access that file.

cwes.name: J2EE Bad Practices: Use of System.exit()
cwes.description: A J2EE application uses System.exit(), which also shuts down its container.

cwes.name: J2EE Bad Practices: Direct Use of Threads
cwes.description: Thread management in a Web application is forbidden in some circumstances and is always highly error prone.

cwes.name: Session Fixation
cwes.description: Authenticating a user, or otherwise establishing a new user session, without invalidating any existing session identifier gives an attacker the opportunity to steal authenticated sessions.

cwes.name: Covert Timing Channel
cwes.description: Covert timing channels convey information by modulating some aspect of system behavior over time, so that the program receiving the information can observe system behavior and infer protected information.

cwes.name: Symbolic Name not Mapping to Correct Object
cwes.description: A constant symbolic reference to an object is used, even though the reference can resolve to a different object over time.

cwes.name: Detection of Error Condition Without Action
cwes.description: The product detects a specific error, but takes no actions to handle the error.

cwes.name: Unchecked Error Condition
cwes.description: [PLANNED FOR DEPRECATION. SEE MAINTENANCE NOTES AND CONSIDER CWE-252, CWE-248, OR CWE-1069.] Ignoring exceptions and other error conditions may allow an attacker to induce unexpected behavior unnoticed.

cwes.name: Missing Report of Error Condition
cwes.description: The product encounters an error but does not provide a status code or return value to indicate that an error has occurred.

cwes.name: Return of Wrong Status Code
cwes.description: A function or operation returns an incorrect return value or status code that does not indicate the true result of execution, causing the product to modify its behavior based on the incorrect result.

cwes.name: Unexpected Status Code or Return Value
cwes.description: The product does not properly check when a function or operation returns a value that is legitimate for the function, but is not expected by the product.

cwes.name: Use of NullPointerException Catch to Detect NULL Pointer Dereference
cwes.description: Catching NullPointerException should not be used as an alternative to programmatic checks to prevent dereferencing a null pointer.

cwes.name: Declaration of Catch for Generic Exception
cwes.description: Catching overly broad exceptions promotes complex error handling code that is more likely to contain security vulnerabilities.

cwes.name: Declaration of Throws for Generic Exception
cwes.description: The product throws or raises an overly broad exceptions that can hide important details and produce inappropriate responses to certain conditions.

cwes.name: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption
cwes.description: The product does not properly control the allocation and maintenance of a limited resource.
