cwes.name: Transmission of Private Resources into a New Sphere ('Resource Leak')
cwes.description: The product makes resources available to untrusted parties when those resources are only intended to be accessed by the product.

cwes.name: Exposure of File Descriptor to Unintended Control Sphere ('File Descriptor Leak')
cwes.description: A process does not close sensitive file descriptors before invoking a child process, which allows the child to perform unauthorized I/O operations using those descriptors.

cwes.name: Improper Resource Shutdown or Release
cwes.description: The product does not release or incorrectly releases a resource before it is made available for re-use.

cwes.name: Asymmetric Resource Consumption (Amplification)
cwes.description: The product does not properly control situations in which an adversary can cause the product to consume or produce excessive resources without requiring the adversary to invest equivalent work or otherwise prove authorization, i.e., the adversary's influence is "asymmetric."

cwes.name: Insufficient Control of Network Message Volume (Network Amplification)
cwes.description: The product does not sufficiently monitor or control transmitted network traffic volume, so that an actor can cause the product to transmit more traffic than should be allowed for that actor.

cwes.name: Inefficient Algorithmic Complexity
cwes.description: An algorithm in a product has an inefficient worst-case computational complexity that may be detrimental to system performance and can be triggered by an attacker, typically using crafted manipulations that ensure that the worst case is being reached.

cwes.name: Incorrect Behavior Order: Early Amplification
cwes.description: The product allows an entity to perform a legitimate but expensive operation before authentication or authorization has taken place.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles a compressed input with a very high compression ratio that produces a large output.

cwes.name: Insufficient Resource Pool
cwes.description: The product's resource pool is not large enough to handle peak demand, which allows an attacker to prevent others from accessing the resource by using a (relatively) large number of requests for resources.

cwes.name: Unrestricted Externally Accessible Lock
cwes.description: The product properly checks for the existence of a lock, but the lock can be externally controlled or influenced by an actor that is outside of the intended sphere of control.

cwes.name: Improper Resource Locking
cwes.description: The product does not lock or does not correctly lock a resource when the product must have exclusive access to the resource.

cwes.name: Missing Lock Check
cwes.description: A product does not check to see if a lock is present before performing sensitive operations on a resource.

cwes.name: Use After Free
cwes.description: The product reuses or references memory after it has been freed. At some point afterward, the memory may be allocated again and saved in another pointer, while the original pointer references a location somewhere within the new allocation. Any operations using the original pointer are no longer vali…

cwes.name: Unprotected Primary Channel
cwes.description: The product uses a primary channel for administration or restricted functionality, but it does not properly protect the channel.

cwes.name: Unprotected Alternate Channel
cwes.description: The product protects a primary channel, but it does not use the same level of protection for an alternate channel.

cwes.name: Race Condition During Access to Alternate Channel
cwes.description: The product opens an alternate channel to communicate with an authorized user, but the channel is accessible to other actors.

cwes.name: Unprotected Windows Messaging Channel ('Shatter')
cwes.description: The product does not properly verify the source of a message in the Windows Messaging System while running at elevated privileges, creating an alternate channel through which an attacker can directly send a message to the product.

cwes.name: Improper Protection of Alternate Path
cwes.description: The product does not sufficiently protect all possible paths that a user can take to access restricted functionality or resources.

cwes.name: Direct Request ('Forced Browsing')
cwes.description: The web application does not adequately enforce appropriate authorization on all restricted URLs, scripts, or files.

cwes.name: Untrusted Search Path
cwes.description: The product searches for critical resources using an externally-supplied search path that can point to resources that are not under the product's direct control.

cwes.name: Uncontrolled Search Path Element
cwes.description: The product uses a fixed or controlled search path to find resources, but one or more locations in that path can be under the control of unintended actors.

cwes.name: Unquoted Search Path or Element
cwes.description: The product uses a search path that contains an unquoted element, in which the element contains whitespace or other separators. This can cause the product to access resources in a parent path.

cwes.name: Deployment of Wrong Handler
cwes.description: The wrong "handler" is assigned to process an object.

cwes.name: Missing Handler
cwes.description: A handler is not available or implemented.

cwes.name: Dangerous Signal Handler not Disabled During Sensitive Operations
cwes.description: The product uses a signal handler that shares state with other signal handlers, but it does not properly mask or prevent those signal handlers from being invoked while the original signal handler is still running.

cwes.name: Unparsed Raw Web Content Delivery
cwes.description: The product stores raw content or supporting code under the web document root with an extension that is not specifically handled by the server.

cwes.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
cwes.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.

cwes.name: Improper Interaction Between Multiple Correctly-Behaving Entities
cwes.description: An interaction error occurs when two entities have correct behavior when running independently of each other, but when they are integrated as components in a larger system or process, they introduce incorrect behaviors that may cause resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Interpretation Conflict
cwes.description: Product A handles inputs or steps differently than Product B, which causes A to perform incorrect actions based on its perception of B's state.

cwes.name: Incomplete Model of Endpoint Features
cwes.description: A product acts as an intermediary or monitor between two or more endpoints, but it does not have a complete model of an endpoint's features, behaviors, or state, potentially causing the product to perform incorrect actions based on this incomplete model.

cwes.name: Behavioral Change in New Version or Environment
cwes.description: A's behavior or functionality changes with a new version of A, or a new environment, which is not known (or manageable) by B.

cwes.name: Expected Behavior Violation
cwes.description: A feature, API, or function does not perform according to its specification.

cwes.name: Unintended Proxy or Intermediary ('Confused Deputy')
cwes.description: The product receives a request, message, or directive from an upstream component, but the product does not sufficiently preserve the original source of the request before forwarding the request to an external actor that is outside of the product's control sphere. This causes the product to appear to…

cwes.name: Inconsistent Interpretation of HTTP Requests ('HTTP Request/Response Smuggling')
cwes.description:
The product acts as an intermediary HTTP agent
         (such as a proxy or firewall) in the data flow between two
         entities such as a client and server, but it does not
         interpret malformed HTTP requests or responses in ways that
         are consistent with how the messages will be…

cwes.name: UI Discrepancy for Security Feature
cwes.description: The user interface does not correctly enable or configure a security feature, but the interface provides feedback that causes the user to believe that the feature is in a secure state.

cwes.name: Unimplemented or Unsupported Feature in UI
cwes.description: A UI function for a security feature appears to be supported and gives feedback to the user that suggests that it is supported, but the underlying functionality is not implemented.

cwes.name: Obsolete Feature in UI
cwes.description: A UI function is obsolete and the product does not warn the user.

cwes.name: The UI Performs the Wrong Action
cwes.description: The UI performs the wrong action with respect to the user's request.

cwes.name: Multiple Interpretations of UI Input
cwes.description: The UI has multiple interpretations of user input but does not prompt the user when it selects the less secure interpretation.

cwes.name: User Interface (UI) Misrepresentation of Critical Information
cwes.description: The user interface (UI) does not properly represent critical information to the user, allowing the information - or its source - to be obscured or spoofed. This is often a component in phishing attacks.

cwes.name: Insecure Default Variable Initialization
cwes.description: The product, by default, initializes an internal variable with an insecure or less secure value than is possible.

cwes.name: External Initialization of Trusted Variables or Data Stores
cwes.description: The product initializes critical internal variables or data stores using inputs that can be modified by untrusted actors.

cwes.name: Non-exit on Failed Initialization
cwes.description: The product does not exit or otherwise modify its operation when security-relevant errors occur during initialization, such as when a configuration file has a format error or a hardware security module (HSM) cannot be activated, which can cause the product to execute in a less secure fashion than in…

cwes.name: Missing Initialization of a Variable
cwes.description: The product does not initialize critical variables, which causes the execution environment to use unexpected values.

cwes.name: Use of Uninitialized Variable
cwes.description: The code uses a variable that has not been initialized, leading to unpredictable or unintended results.

cwes.name: Incomplete Cleanup
cwes.description: The product does not properly "clean up" and remove temporary or supporting resources after they have been used.

cwes.name: Improper Cleanup on Thrown Exception
cwes.description: The product does not clean up its state or incorrectly cleans up its state when an exception is thrown, leading to unexpected state or control flow.

cwes.name: Duplicate Key in Associative List (Alist)
cwes.description: Duplicate keys in associative lists can lead to non-unique keys being mistaken for an error.

cwes.name: Use of Externally-Controlled Input to Select Classes or Code ('Unsafe Reflection')
cwes.description: The product uses external input with reflection to select which classes or code to use, but it does not sufficiently prevent the input from selecting improper classes or code.

cwes.name: Modification of Assumed-Immutable Data (MAID)
cwes.description: The product does not properly protect an assumed-immutable element from being modified by an attacker.

cwes.name: External Control of Assumed-Immutable Web Parameter
cwes.description: The web application does not sufficiently verify inputs that are assumed to be immutable but are actually externally controllable, such as hidden form fields.

cwes.name: PHP External Variable Modification
cwes.description: A PHP application does not properly protect against the modification of variables from external sources, such as query parameters or cookies. This can expose the application to numerous weaknesses that would not exist otherwise.

cwes.name: Use of Function with Inconsistent Implementations
cwes.description: The code uses a function that has inconsistent implementations across operating systems and versions.

cwes.name: Undefined Behavior for Input to API
cwes.description: The behavior of this function is undefined unless its control parameter is set to a specific value.

cwes.name: NULL Pointer Dereference
cwes.description: The product dereferences a pointer that it expects to be valid but is NULL.

cwes.name: Use of Obsolete Function
cwes.description: The code uses deprecated or obsolete functions, which suggests that the code has not been actively reviewed or maintained.

cwes.name: Missing Default Case in Multiple Condition Expression
cwes.description: The code does not have a default case in an expression with multiple conditions, such as a switch statement.

cwes.name: Use of Incorrect Operator
cwes.description: The product accidentally uses the wrong operator, which changes the logic in security-relevant ways.

cwes.name: Assigning instead of Comparing
cwes.description: The code uses an operator for assignment when the intention was to perform a comparison.

cwes.name: Omitted Break Statement in Switch
cwes.description: The product omits a break statement within a switch or similar construct, causing code associated with multiple conditions to execute. This can cause problems when the programmer only intended to execute code associated with one condition.

cwes.name: Comparison of Classes by Name
cwes.description: The product compares classes by name, which can cause it to use the wrong class when multiple classes can have the same name.

cwes.name: Reliance on Package-level Scope
cwes.description: Java packages are not inherently closed; therefore, relying on them for code security is not a good practice.

cwes.name: Exposure of Data Element to Wrong Session
cwes.description: The product does not sufficiently enforce boundaries between the states of different sessions, causing data to be provided to, or used by, the wrong session.

cwes.name: Active Debug Code
cwes.description: The product is released with debugging code still enabled or active.

cwes.name: Public cloneable() Method Without Final ('Object Hijack')
cwes.description: A class has a cloneable() method that is not declared final, which allows an object to be created without calling the constructor. This can cause the object to be in an unexpected state.

cwes.name: Use of Inner Class Containing Sensitive Data
cwes.description: Inner classes are translated into classes that are accessible at package scope and may expose code that the programmer intended to keep private to attackers.

cwes.name: Critical Public Variable Without Final Modifier
cwes.description: The product has a critical public variable that is not final, which allows the variable to be modified to contain unexpected values.

cwes.name: Download of Code Without Integrity Check
cwes.description: The product downloads source code or an executable from a remote location and executes the code without sufficiently verifying the origin and integrity of the code.

cwes.name: Private Data Structure Returned From A Public Method
cwes.description: The product has a method that is declared public, but returns a reference to a private data structure, which could then be modified in unexpected ways.

cwes.name: Public Data Assigned to Private Array-Typed Field
cwes.description: Assigning public data to a private array is equivalent to giving public access to the array.

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive System Information to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
cwes.description: The product does not properly prevent sensitive system-level information from being accessed by unauthorized actors who do not have the same level of access to the underlying system as the product does.

cwes.name: Cloneable Class Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The code contains a class with sensitive data, but the class is cloneable. The data can then be accessed by cloning the class.

cwes.name: Serializable Class Containing Sensitive Data
cwes.description: The code contains a class with sensitive data, but the class does not explicitly deny serialization. The data can be accessed by serializing the class through another class.

cwes.name: Public Static Field Not Marked Final
cwes.description: An object contains a public static field that is not marked final, which might allow it to be modified in unexpected ways.

cwes.name: Trust Boundary Violation
cwes.description: The product mixes trusted and untrusted data in the same data structure or structured message.

cwes.name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
cwes.description: The product deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently ensuring that the resulting data will be valid.

cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.

cwes.name: Trojan Horse
cwes.description: The product appears to contain benign or useful functionality, but it also contains code that is hidden from normal operation that violates the intended security policy of the user or the system administrator.

cwes.name: Non-Replicating Malicious Code
cwes.description: Non-replicating malicious code only resides on the target system or product that is attacked; it does not attempt to spread to other systems.

cwes.name: Replicating Malicious Code (Virus or Worm)
cwes.description: Replicating malicious code, including viruses and worms, will attempt to attack other systems once it has successfully compromised the target system or the product.

cwes.name: Trapdoor
cwes.description: A trapdoor is a hidden piece of code that responds to a special input, allowing its user access to resources without passing through the normal security enforcement mechanism.

cwes.name: Logic/Time Bomb
cwes.description: The product contains code that is designed to disrupt the legitimate operation of the product (or its environment) when a certain time passes, or when a certain logical condition is met.

cwes.name: Spyware
cwes.description: The product collects personally identifiable information about a human user or the user's activities, but the product accesses this information using other resources besides itself, and it does not require that user's explicit approval or direct input into the product.

cwes.name: Covert Channel
cwes.description: A covert channel is a path that can be used to transfer information in a way not intended by the system's designers.

cwes.name: Covert Storage Channel
cwes.description: A covert storage channel transfers information through the setting of bits by one program and the reading of those bits by another. What distinguishes this case from that of ordinary operation is that the bits are used to convey encoded information.

cwes.name: .NET Misconfiguration: Use of Impersonation
cwes.description: Allowing a .NET application to run at potentially escalated levels of access to the underlying operating and file systems can be dangerous and result in various forms of attacks.

cwes.name: Weak Password Requirements
cwes.description: The product does not require that users should have strong passwords.

cwes.name: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
cwes.description: The product transmits or stores authentication credentials, but it uses an insecure method that is susceptible to unauthorized interception and/or retrieval.

cwes.name: Unprotected Transport of Credentials
cwes.description: Login pages do not use adequate measures to protect the user name and password while they are in transit from the client to the server.

cwes.name: Use of Cache Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The code uses a cache that contains sensitive information, but the cache can be read by an actor outside of the intended control sphere.

cwes.name: Use of Web Browser Cache Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The web application does not use an appropriate caching policy that specifies the extent to which each web page and associated form fields should be cached.

cwes.name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information in an Environment Variable
cwes.description: The product uses an environment variable to store unencrypted sensitive information.

cwes.name: Exposure of Version-Control Repository to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
cwes.description: The product stores a CVS, git, or other repository in a directory, archive, or other resource that is stored, transferred, or otherwise made accessible to unauthorized actors.

cwes.name: Exposure of Core Dump File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
cwes.description: The product generates a core dump file in a directory, archive, or other resource that is stored, transferred, or otherwise made accessible to unauthorized actors.

cwes.name: Exposure of Access Control List Files to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
cwes.description: The product stores access control list files in a directory or other container that is accessible to actors outside of the intended control sphere.

cwes.name: Exposure of Backup File to an Unauthorized Control Sphere
cwes.description: A backup file is stored in a directory or archive that is made accessible to unauthorized actors.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Test Code
cwes.description: Accessible test applications can pose a variety of security risks. Since developers or administrators rarely consider that someone besides themselves would even know about the existence of these applications, it is common for them to contain sensitive information or functions.

cwes.name: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
cwes.description: The product writes sensitive information to a log file.

cwes.name: Exposure of Information Through Shell Error Message
cwes.description: A command shell error message indicates that there exists an unhandled exception in the web application code. In many cases, an attacker can leverage the conditions that cause these errors in order to gain unauthorized access to the system.

cwes.name: Servlet Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: A servlet error message indicates that there exists an unhandled exception in the web application code and may provide useful information to an attacker.

cwes.name: Java Runtime Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: In many cases, an attacker can leverage the conditions that cause unhandled exception errors in order to gain unauthorized access to the system.

cwes.name: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Externally-Accessible File or Directory
cwes.description: The product places sensitive information into files or directories that are accessible to actors who are allowed to have access to the files, but not to the sensitive information.

cwes.name: Use of Persistent Cookies Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The web application uses persistent cookies, but the cookies contain sensitive information.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code
cwes.description: Source code on a web server or repository often contains sensitive information and should generally not be accessible to users.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in an Include File
cwes.description: If an include file source is accessible, the file can contain usernames and passwords, as well as sensitive information pertaining to the application and system.

cwes.name: Use of Singleton Pattern Without Synchronization in a Multithreaded Context
cwes.description: The product uses the singleton pattern when creating a resource within a multithreaded environment.

cwes.name: Missing Standardized Error Handling Mechanism
cwes.description: The product does not use a standardized method for handling errors throughout the code, which might introduce inconsistent error handling and resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Suspicious Comment
cwes.description: The code contains comments that suggest the presence of bugs, incomplete functionality, or weaknesses.

cwes.name: Use of Hard-coded, Security-relevant Constants
cwes.description: The product uses hard-coded constants instead of symbolic names for security-critical values, which increases the likelihood of mistakes during code maintenance or security policy change.

cwes.name: Exposure of Information Through Directory Listing
cwes.description: The product inappropriately exposes a directory listing with an index of all the resources located inside of the directory.

cwes.name: Missing Password Field Masking
cwes.description: The product does not mask passwords during entry, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture passwords.

cwes.name: Server-generated Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: Certain conditions, such as network failure, will cause a server error message to be displayed.

cwes.name: Incorrect Behavior Order: Authorization Before Parsing and Canonicalization
cwes.description: If a web server does not fully parse requested URLs before it examines them for authorization, it may be possible for an attacker to bypass authorization protection.

cwes.name: Files or Directories Accessible to External Parties
cwes.description: The product makes files or directories accessible to unauthorized actors, even though they should not be.

cwes.name: Command Shell in Externally Accessible Directory
cwes.description: A possible shell file exists in /cgi-bin/ or other accessible directories. This is extremely dangerous and can be used by an attacker to execute commands on the web server.

cwes.name: J2EE Misconfiguration: Plaintext Password in Configuration File
cwes.description: The J2EE application stores a plaintext password in a configuration file.

cwes.name: Dead Code
cwes.description: The product contains dead code, which can never be executed.

cwes.name: Assignment to Variable without Use
cwes.description: The variable's value is assigned but never used, making it a dead store.

cwes.name: SQL Injection: Hibernate
cwes.description: Using Hibernate to execute a dynamic SQL statement built with user-controlled input can allow an attacker to modify the statement's meaning or to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

cwes.name: Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking
cwes.description: The product relies on the existence or values of cookies when performing security-critical operations, but it does not properly ensure that the setting is valid for the associated user.

cwes.name: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled SQL Primary Key
cwes.description: The product uses a database table that includes records that should not be accessible to an actor, but it executes a SQL statement with a primary key that can be controlled by that actor.

cwes.name: Unsynchronized Access to Shared Data in a Multithreaded Context
cwes.description: The product does not properly synchronize shared data, such as static variables across threads, which can lead to undefined behavior and unpredictable data changes.

cwes.name: finalize() Method Without super.finalize()
cwes.description: The product contains a finalize() method that does not call super.finalize().

cwes.name: Expression is Always False
cwes.description: The product contains an expression that will always evaluate to false.

cwes.name: Expression is Always True
cwes.description: The product contains an expression that will always evaluate to true.

cwes.name: Call to Thread run() instead of start()
cwes.description: The product calls a thread's run() method instead of calling start(), which causes the code to run in the thread of the caller instead of the callee.

cwes.name: Improper Following of Specification by Caller
cwes.description: The product does not follow or incorrectly follows the specifications as required by the implementation language, environment, framework, protocol, or platform.

cwes.name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Synchronization Primitives
cwes.description: The product violates the Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB) specification by using thread synchronization primitives.

cwes.name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of AWT Swing
cwes.description: The product violates the Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB) specification by using AWT/Swing.

cwes.name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Java I/O
cwes.description: The product violates the Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB) specification by using the java.io package.

cwes.name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Sockets
cwes.description: The product violates the Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB) specification by using sockets.

cwes.name: EJB Bad Practices: Use of Class Loader
cwes.description: The product violates the Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB) specification by using the class loader.

cwes.name: J2EE Bad Practices: Non-serializable Object Stored in Session
cwes.description: The product stores a non-serializable object as an HttpSession attribute, which can hurt reliability.

cwes.name: clone() Method Without super.clone()
cwes.description: The product contains a clone() method that does not call super.clone() to obtain the new object.

cwes.name: Object Model Violation: Just One of Equals and Hashcode Defined
cwes.description: The product does not maintain equal hashcodes for equal objects.

cwes.name: Array Declared Public, Final, and Static
cwes.description: The product declares an array public, final, and static, which is not sufficient to prevent the array's contents from being modified.

cwes.name: finalize() Method Declared Public
cwes.description: The product violates secure coding principles for mobile code by declaring a finalize() method public.

cwes.name: Return Inside Finally Block
cwes.description: The code has a return statement inside a finally block, which will cause any thrown exception in the try block to be discarded.

cwes.name: Empty Synchronized Block
cwes.description: The product contains an empty synchronized block.

cwes.name: Explicit Call to Finalize()
cwes.description: The product makes an explicit call to the finalize() method from outside the finalizer.

cwes.name: Call to Non-ubiquitous API
cwes.description: The product uses an API function that does not exist on all versions of the target platform. This could cause portability problems or inconsistencies that allow denial of service or other consequences.

cwes.name: Sensitive Data Storage in Improperly Locked Memory
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive data in memory that is not locked, or that has been incorrectly locked, which might cause the memory to be written to swap files on disk by the virtual memory manager. This can make the data more accessible to external actors.

cwes.name: Authentication Bypass: OpenSSL CTX Object Modified after SSL Objects are Created
cwes.description: The product modifies the SSL context after connection creation has begun.

cwes.name: J2EE Framework: Saving Unserializable Objects to Disk
cwes.description: When the J2EE container attempts to write unserializable objects to disk there is no guarantee that the process will complete successfully.

cwes.name: Comparison of Object References Instead of Object Contents
cwes.description: The product compares object references instead of the contents of the objects themselves, preventing it from detecting equivalent objects.

cwes.name: Use of Wrong Operator in String Comparison
cwes.description: The product uses the wrong operator when comparing a string, such as using "==" when the .equals() method should be used instead.

cwes.name: Use of GET Request Method With Sensitive Query Strings
cwes.description: The web application uses the HTTP GET method to process a request and includes sensitive information in the query string of that request.

cwes.name: Missing Validation of OpenSSL Certificate
cwes.description: The product uses OpenSSL and trusts or uses a certificate without using the SSL_get_verify_result() function to ensure that the certificate satisfies all necessary security requirements.

cwes.name: Uncaught Exception in Servlet
cwes.description: The Servlet does not catch all exceptions, which may reveal sensitive debugging information.

cwes.name: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
cwes.description: The web application accepts a user-controlled input that specifies a link to an external site, and uses that link in a redirect.

cwes.name: Client-Side Enforcement of Server-Side Security
cwes.description: The product is composed of a server that relies on the client to implement a mechanism that is intended to protect the server.

cwes.name: Use of Client-Side Authentication
cwes.description: A client/server product performs authentication within client code but not in server code, allowing server-side authentication to be bypassed via a modified client that omits the authentication check.

cwes.name: Multiple Binds to the Same Port
cwes.description: When multiple sockets are allowed to bind to the same port, other services on that port may be stolen or spoofed.

cwes.name: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
cwes.description: The product does not properly check inputs that are used for loop conditions, potentially leading to a denial of service or other consequences because of excessive looping.

cwes.name: Public Static Final Field References Mutable Object
cwes.description: A public or protected static final field references a mutable object, which allows the object to be changed by malicious code, or accidentally from another package.

cwes.name: Struts: Non-private Field in ActionForm Class
cwes.description: An ActionForm class contains a field that has not been declared private, which can be accessed without using a setter or getter.

cwes.name: Double-Checked Locking
cwes.description: The product uses double-checked locking to access a resource without the overhead of explicit synchronization, but the locking is insufficient.

cwes.name: Externally Controlled Reference to a Resource in Another Sphere
cwes.description: The product uses an externally controlled name or reference that resolves to a resource that is outside of the intended control sphere.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
cwes.description: The product processes an XML document that can contain XML entities with URIs that resolve to documents outside of the intended sphere of control, causing the product to embed incorrect documents into its output.

cwes.name: Improper Authorization of Index Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The product creates a search index of private or sensitive documents, but it does not properly limit index access to actors who are authorized to see the original information.

cwes.name: Insufficient Session Expiration
cwes.description: According to WASC, "Insufficient Session Expiration is when a web site permits an attacker to reuse old session credentials or session IDs for authorization."

cwes.name: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute
cwes.description: The Secure attribute for sensitive cookies in HTTPS sessions is not set.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Sensitive Information in Source Code Comments
cwes.description: While adding general comments is very useful, some programmers tend to leave important data, such as: filenames related to the web application, old links or links which were not meant to be browsed by users, old code fragments, etc.

cwes.name: Incomplete Identification of Uploaded File Variables (PHP)
cwes.description: The PHP application uses an old method for processing uploaded files by referencing the four global variables that are set for each file (e.g. $varname, $varname_size, $varname_name, $varname_type). These variables could be overwritten by attackers, causing the application to process unauthorized fi…

cwes.name: Reachable Assertion
cwes.description: The product contains an assert() or similar statement that can be triggered by an attacker, which leads to an application exit or other behavior that is more severe than necessary.

cwes.name: Exposed Unsafe ActiveX Method
cwes.description: An ActiveX control is intended for use in a web browser, but it exposes dangerous methods that perform actions that are outside of the browser's security model (e.g. the zone or domain).

cwes.name: Unverified Password Change
cwes.description: When setting a new password for a user, the product does not require knowledge of the original password, or using another form of authentication.

cwes.name: Variable Extraction Error
cwes.description: The product uses external input to determine the names of variables into which information is extracted, without verifying that the names of the specified variables are valid. This could cause the program to overwrite unintended variables.

cwes.name: Improper Validation of Function Hook Arguments
cwes.description: The product adds hooks to user-accessible API functions, but it does not properly validate the arguments. This could lead to resultant vulnerabilities.

cwes.name: Unsafe ActiveX Control Marked Safe For Scripting
cwes.description: An ActiveX control is intended for restricted use, but it has been marked as safe-for-scripting.

cwes.name: Executable Regular Expression Error
cwes.description: The product uses a regular expression that either (1) contains an executable component with user-controlled inputs, or (2) allows a user to enable execution by inserting pattern modifiers.

cwes.name: Permissive Regular Expression
cwes.description: The product uses a regular expression that does not sufficiently restrict the set of allowed values.

cwes.name: Null Byte Interaction Error (Poison Null Byte)
cwes.description: The product does not properly handle null bytes or NUL characters when passing data between different representations or components.

cwes.name: Dynamic Variable Evaluation
cwes.description: In a language where the user can influence the name of a variable at runtime, if the variable names are not controlled, an attacker can read or write to arbitrary variables, or access arbitrary functions.

cwes.name: Function Call with Incorrectly Specified Arguments
cwes.description: The product calls a function, procedure, or routine with arguments that are not correctly specified, leading to always-incorrect behavior and resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Not Failing Securely ('Failing Open')
cwes.description: When the product encounters an error condition or failure, its design requires it to fall back to a state that is less secure than other options that are available, such as selecting the weakest encryption algorithm or using the most permissive access control restrictions.

cwes.name: Unnecessary Complexity in Protection Mechanism (Not Using 'Economy of Mechanism')
cwes.description: The product uses a more complex mechanism than necessary, which could lead to resultant weaknesses when the mechanism is not correctly understood, modeled, configured, implemented, or used.

cwes.name: Not Using Complete Mediation
cwes.description: The product does not perform access checks on a resource every time the resource is accessed by an entity, which can create resultant weaknesses if that entity's rights or privileges change over time.

cwes.name: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
cwes.description: The system's authorization functionality does not prevent one user from gaining access to another user's data or record by modifying the key value identifying the data.

cwes.name: Weak Password Recovery Mechanism for Forgotten Password
cwes.description: The product contains a mechanism for users to recover or change their passwords without knowing the original password, but the mechanism is weak.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of Names for Files and Other Resources
cwes.description: The product constructs the name of a file or other resource using input from an upstream component, but it does not restrict or incorrectly restricts the resulting name.

cwes.name: External Control of Critical State Data
cwes.description: The product stores security-critical state information about its users, or the product itself, in a location that is accessible to unauthorized actors.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions ('XPath Injection')
cwes.description: The product uses external input to dynamically construct an XPath expression used to retrieve data from an XML database, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes that input. This allows an attacker to control the structure of the query.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of HTTP Headers for Scripting Syntax
cwes.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes web scripting syntax in HTTP headers that can be used by web browser components that can process raw headers, such as Flash.

cwes.name: Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism
cwes.description: The product contains an account lockout protection mechanism, but the mechanism is too restrictive and can be triggered too easily, which allows attackers to deny service to legitimate users by causing their accounts to be locked out.

cwes.name: Reliance on File Name or Extension of Externally-Supplied File
cwes.description: The product allows a file to be uploaded, but it relies on the file name or extension of the file to determine the appropriate behaviors. This could be used by attackers to cause the file to be misclassified and processed in a dangerous fashion.

cwes.name: Use of Non-Canonical URL Paths for Authorization Decisions
cwes.description: The product defines policy namespaces and makes authorization decisions based on the assumption that a URL is canonical. This can allow a non-canonical URL to bypass the authorization.

cwes.name: Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs
cwes.description: The product does not conform to the API requirements for a function call that requires extra privileges. This could allow attackers to gain privileges by causing the function to be called incorrectly.

cwes.name: Reliance on Obfuscation or Encryption of Security-Relevant Inputs without Integrity Checking
cwes.description: The product uses obfuscation or encryption of inputs that should not be mutable by an external actor, but the product does not use integrity checks to detect if those inputs have been modified.

cwes.name: Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side
cwes.description: The server contains a protection mechanism that assumes that any URI that is accessed using HTTP GET will not cause a state change to the associated resource. This might allow attackers to bypass intended access restrictions and conduct resource modification and deletion attacks, since some applicat…

cwes.name: Exposure of WSDL File Containing Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The Web services architecture may require exposing a Web Service Definition Language (WSDL) file that contains information on the publicly accessible services and how callers of these services should interact with them (e.g. what parameters they expect and what types they return).

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Data within XQuery Expressions ('XQuery Injection')
cwes.description: The product uses external input to dynamically construct an XQuery expression used to retrieve data from an XML database, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes that input. This allows an attacker to control the structure of the query.

cwes.name: Improper Isolation or Compartmentalization
cwes.description: The product does not properly compartmentalize or isolate functionality, processes, or resources that require different privilege levels, rights, or permissions.

cwes.name: Reliance on a Single Factor in a Security Decision
cwes.description: A protection mechanism relies exclusively, or to a large extent, on the evaluation of a single condition or the integrity of a single object or entity in order to make a decision about granting access to restricted resources or functionality.

cwes.name: Insufficient Psychological Acceptability
cwes.description: The product has a protection mechanism that is too difficult or inconvenient to use, encouraging non-malicious users to disable or bypass the mechanism, whether by accident or on purpose.

cwes.name: Reliance on Security Through Obscurity
cwes.description: The product uses a protection mechanism whose strength depends heavily on its obscurity, such that knowledge of its algorithms or key data is sufficient to defeat the mechanism.

cwes.name: Violation of Secure Design Principles
cwes.description: The product violates well-established principles for secure design.

cwes.name: Improper Synchronization
cwes.description: The product utilizes multiple threads, processes, components, or systems to allow temporary access to a shared resource that can only be exclusive to one process at a time, but it does not properly synchronize these actions, which might cause simultaneous accesses of this resource by multiple thread…

cwes.name: Use of a Non-reentrant Function in a Concurrent Context
cwes.description: The product calls a non-reentrant function in a concurrent context in which a competing code sequence (e.g. thread or signal handler) may have an opportunity to call the same function or otherwise influence its state.

cwes.name: Improper Control of a Resource Through its Lifetime
cwes.description: The product does not maintain or incorrectly maintains control over a resource throughout its lifetime of creation, use, and release.

cwes.name: Operation on Resource in Wrong Phase of Lifetime
cwes.description: The product performs an operation on a resource at the wrong phase of the resource's lifecycle, which can lead to unexpected behaviors.

cwes.name: Improper Locking
cwes.description: The product does not properly acquire or release a lock on a resource, leading to unexpected resource state changes and behaviors.

cwes.name: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere
cwes.description: The product exposes a resource to the wrong control sphere, providing unintended actors with inappropriate access to the resource.

cwes.name: Incorrect Resource Transfer Between Spheres
cwes.description: The product does not properly transfer a resource/behavior to another sphere, or improperly imports a resource/behavior from another sphere, in a manner that provides unintended control over that resource.

cwes.name: Always-Incorrect Control Flow Implementation
cwes.description: The code contains a control flow path that does not reflect the algorithm that the path is intended to implement, leading to incorrect behavior any time this path is navigated.

cwes.name: Lack of Administrator Control over Security
cwes.description: The product uses security features in a way that prevents the product's administrator from tailoring security settings to reflect the environment in which the product is being used. This introduces resultant weaknesses or prevents it from operating at a level of security that is desired by the admin…

cwes.name: Operation on a Resource after Expiration or Release
cwes.description: The product uses, accesses, or otherwise operates on a resource after that resource has been expired, released, or revoked.

cwes.name: External Influence of Sphere Definition
cwes.description: The product does not prevent the definition of control spheres from external actors.

cwes.name: Uncontrolled Recursion
cwes.description: The product does not properly control the amount of recursion that takes place,  consuming excessive resources, such as allocated memory or the program stack.

cwes.name: Multiple Operations on Resource in Single-Operation Context
cwes.description: The product performs the same operation on a resource two or more times, when the operation should only be applied once.

cwes.name: Incorrect Conversion between Numeric Types
cwes.description: When converting from one data type to another, such as long to integer, data can be omitted or translated in a way that produces unexpected values. If the resulting values are used in a sensitive context, then dangerous behaviors may occur.

cwes.name: Incorrect Calculation
cwes.description: The product performs a calculation that generates incorrect or unintended results that are later used in security-critical decisions or resource management.

cwes.name: Function Call With Incorrect Order of Arguments
cwes.description: The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies the arguments in an incorrect order, leading to resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Incorrect Provision of Specified Functionality
cwes.description: The code does not function according to its published specifications, potentially leading to incorrect usage.

cwes.name: Function Call With Incorrect Argument Type
cwes.description: The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies an argument that is the wrong data type, which may lead to resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Function Call With Incorrectly Specified Argument Value
cwes.description: The product calls a function, procedure, or routine, but the caller specifies an argument that contains the wrong value, which may lead to resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Insufficient Control Flow Management
cwes.description: The code does not sufficiently manage its control flow during execution, creating conditions in which the control flow can be modified in unexpected ways.

cwes.name: Incomplete Denylist to Cross-Site Scripting
cwes.description: The product uses a denylist-based protection mechanism to defend against XSS attacks, but the denylist is incomplete, allowing XSS variants to succeed.

cwes.name: Protection Mechanism Failure
cwes.description: The product does not use or incorrectly uses a protection mechanism that provides sufficient defense against directed attacks against the product.

cwes.name: Use of Multiple Resources with Duplicate Identifier
cwes.description: The product uses multiple resources that can have the same identifier, in a context in which unique identifiers are required.

cwes.name: Use of Low-Level Functionality
cwes.description: The product uses low-level functionality that is explicitly prohibited by the framework or specification under which the product is supposed to operate.

cwes.name: Incorrect Behavior Order
cwes.description: The product performs multiple related behaviors, but the behaviors are performed in the wrong order in ways that may produce resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Incorrect Comparison
cwes.description: The product compares two entities in a security-relevant context, but the comparison is incorrect.

cwes.name: Execution After Redirect (EAR)
cwes.description: The web application sends a redirect to another location, but instead of exiting, it executes additional code.

cwes.name: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions
cwes.description: The product does not properly anticipate or handle exceptional conditions that rarely occur during normal operation of the product.

cwes.name: Incorrect Type Conversion or Cast
cwes.description: The product does not correctly convert an object, resource, or structure from one type to a different type.

cwes.name: Incorrect Control Flow Scoping
cwes.description: The product does not properly return control flow to the proper location after it has completed a task or detected an unusual condition.

cwes.name: Use of Incorrectly-Resolved Name or Reference
cwes.description: The product uses a name or reference to access a resource, but the name/reference resolves to a resource that is outside of the intended control sphere.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization
cwes.description: The product does not ensure or incorrectly ensures that structured messages or data are well-formed and that certain security properties are met before being read from an upstream component or sent to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Incorrect Ownership Assignment
cwes.description: The product assigns an owner to a resource, but the owner is outside of the intended control sphere.

cwes.name: Improper Adherence to Coding Standards
cwes.description: The product does not follow certain coding rules for development, which can lead to resultant weaknesses or increase the severity of the associated vulnerabilities.

cwes.name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
cwes.description: The product specifies permissions for a security-critical resource in a way that allows that resource to be read or modified by unintended actors.

cwes.name: Exposed Dangerous Method or Function
cwes.description: The product provides an Applications Programming Interface (API) or similar interface for interaction with external actors, but the interface includes a dangerous method or function that is not properly restricted.

cwes.name: Improper Check for Unusual or Exceptional Conditions
cwes.description: The product does not check or incorrectly checks for unusual or exceptional conditions that are not expected to occur frequently during day to day operation of the product.

cwes.name: Improper Handling of Exceptional Conditions
cwes.description: The product does not handle or incorrectly handles an exceptional condition.

cwes.name: Missing Custom Error Page
cwes.description: The product does not return custom error pages to the user, possibly exposing sensitive information.

cwes.name: Selection of Less-Secure Algorithm During Negotiation ('Algorithm Downgrade')
cwes.description: A protocol or its implementation supports interaction between multiple actors and allows those actors to negotiate which algorithm should be used as a protection mechanism such as encryption or authentication, but it does not select the strongest algorithm that is available to both parties.

cwes.name: Reliance on Undefined, Unspecified, or Implementation-Defined Behavior
cwes.description: The product uses an API function, data structure, or other entity in a way that relies on properties that are not always guaranteed to hold for that entity.

cwes.name: Use of a One-Way Hash without a Salt
cwes.description: The product uses a one-way cryptographic hash against an input that should not be reversible, such as a password, but the product does not also use a salt as part of the input.

cwes.name: Use of a One-Way Hash with a Predictable Salt
cwes.description: The product uses a one-way cryptographic hash against an input that should not be reversible, such as a password, but the product uses a predictable salt as part of the input.

cwes.name: Multiple Locks of a Critical Resource
cwes.description: The product locks a critical resource more times than intended, leading to an unexpected state in the system.

cwes.name: Multiple Unlocks of a Critical Resource
cwes.description: The product unlocks a critical resource more times than intended, leading to an unexpected state in the system.

cwes.name: Critical Data Element Declared Public
cwes.description: The product declares a critical variable, field, or member to be public when intended security policy requires it to be private.

cwes.name: Access to Critical Private Variable via Public Method
cwes.description: The product defines a public method that reads or modifies a private variable.

cwes.name: Incorrect Short Circuit Evaluation
cwes.description: The product contains a conditional statement with multiple logical expressions in which one of the non-leading expressions may produce side effects. This may lead to an unexpected state in the program after the execution of the conditional, because short-circuiting logic may prevent the side effects…

cwes.name: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling
cwes.description: The product allocates a reusable resource or group of resources on behalf of an actor without imposing any intended restrictions on the size or number of resources that can be allocated.

cwes.name: Missing Reference to Active Allocated Resource
cwes.description: The product does not properly maintain a reference to a resource that has been allocated, which prevents the resource from being reclaimed.

cwes.name: Missing Reference to Active File Descriptor or Handle
cwes.description: The product does not properly maintain references to a file descriptor or handle, which prevents that file descriptor/handle from being reclaimed.

cwes.name: Allocation of File Descriptors or Handles Without Limits or Throttling
cwes.description: The product allocates file descriptors or handles on behalf of an actor without imposing any restrictions on how many descriptors can be allocated, in violation of the intended security policy for that actor.

cwes.name: Missing Release of File Descriptor or Handle after Effective Lifetime
cwes.description: The product does not release a file descriptor or handle after its effective lifetime has ended, i.e., after the file descriptor/handle is no longer needed.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')
cwes.description: The product uses XML documents and allows their structure to be defined with a Document Type Definition (DTD), but it does not properly control the number of recursive definitions of entities.

cwes.name: Regular Expression without Anchors
cwes.description: The product uses a regular expression to perform neutralization, but the regular expression is not anchored and may allow malicious or malformed data to slip through.

cwes.name: Insufficient Logging
cwes.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.

cwes.name: Logging of Excessive Data
cwes.description: The product logs too much information, making log files hard to process and possibly hindering recovery efforts or forensic analysis after an attack.

cwes.name: Use of RSA Algorithm without OAEP
cwes.description: The product uses the RSA algorithm but does not incorporate Optimal Asymmetric Encryption Padding (OAEP), which might weaken the encryption.

cwes.name: Operator Precedence Logic Error
cwes.description: The product uses an expression in which operator precedence causes incorrect logic to be used.

cwes.name: Reliance on Cookies without Validation and Integrity Checking in a Security Decision
cwes.description: The product uses a protection mechanism that relies on the existence or values of a cookie, but it does not properly ensure that the cookie is valid for the associated user.

cwes.name: Out-of-bounds Write
cwes.description: The product writes data past the end, or before the beginning, of the intended buffer.

cwes.name: Memory Allocation with Excessive Size Value
cwes.description: The product allocates memory based on an untrusted, large size value, but it does not ensure that the size is within expected limits, allowing arbitrary amounts of memory to be allocated.

cwes.name: Improper Filtering of Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not filter or incorrectly filters special elements before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Incomplete Filtering of Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not completely filter special elements before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Incomplete Filtering of One or More Instances of Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not completely filter one or more instances of special elements before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Only Filtering One Instance of a Special Element
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but only filters a single instance of a special element before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Incomplete Filtering of Multiple Instances of Special Elements
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not filter all instances of a special element before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Only Filtering Special Elements at a Specified Location
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but only accounts for special elements at a specified location, thereby missing remaining special elements that may exist before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Only Filtering Special Elements Relative to a Marker
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but only accounts for special elements positioned relative to a marker (e.g. "at the beginning/end of a string; the second argument"), thereby missing remaining special elements that may exist before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Only Filtering Special Elements at an Absolute Position
cwes.description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but only accounts for special elements at an absolute position (e.g. "byte number 10"), thereby missing remaining special elements that may exist before sending it to a downstream component.

cwes.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
cwes.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.

cwes.name: Improper Control of Interaction Frequency
cwes.description: The product does not properly limit the number or frequency of interactions that it has with an actor, such as the number of incoming requests.

cwes.name: Guessable CAPTCHA
cwes.description: The product uses a CAPTCHA challenge, but the challenge can be guessed or automatically recognized by a non-human actor.

cwes.name: Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision
cwes.description: The product uses a protection mechanism that relies on the existence or values of an input, but the input can be modified by an untrusted actor in a way that bypasses the protection mechanism.

cwes.name: Missing Synchronization
cwes.description: The product utilizes a shared resource in a concurrent manner but does not attempt to synchronize access to the resource.

cwes.name: Incorrect Synchronization
cwes.description: The product utilizes a shared resource in a concurrent manner, but it does not correctly synchronize access to the resource.

cwes.name: Premature Release of Resource During Expected Lifetime
cwes.description: The product releases a resource that is still intended to be used by itself or another actor.

cwes.name: Signal Handler with Functionality that is not Asynchronous-Safe
cwes.description: The product defines a signal handler that contains code sequences that are not asynchronous-safe, i.e., the functionality is not reentrant, or it can be interrupted.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
cwes.description: The product imports, requires, or includes executable functionality (such as a library) from a source that is outside of the intended control sphere.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Web Functionality from an Untrusted Source
cwes.description: The product includes web functionality (such as a web widget) from another domain, which causes it to operate within the domain of the product, potentially granting total access and control of the product to the untrusted source.

cwes.name: Signal Handler Function Associated with Multiple Signals
cwes.description: The product defines a function that is used as a handler for more than one signal.

cwes.name: Unlock of a Resource that is not Locked
cwes.description: The product attempts to unlock a resource that is not locked.

cwes.name: Deadlock
cwes.description: The product contains multiple threads or executable segments that are waiting for each other to release a necessary lock, resulting in deadlock.

cwes.name: Excessive Iteration
cwes.description: The product performs an iteration or loop without sufficiently limiting the number of times that the loop is executed.

cwes.name: Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')
cwes.description: The product contains an iteration or loop with an exit condition that cannot be reached, i.e., an infinite loop.

cwes.name: Use of Password Hash Instead of Password for Authentication
cwes.description: The product records password hashes in a data store, receives a hash of a password from a client, and compares the supplied hash to the hash obtained from the data store.

cwes.name: Improper Enforcement of a Single, Unique Action
cwes.description: The product requires that an actor should only be able to perform an action once, or to have only one unique action, but the product does not enforce or improperly enforces this restriction.

cwes.name: Inappropriate Encoding for Output Context
cwes.description: The product uses or specifies an encoding when generating output to a downstream component, but the specified encoding is not the same as the encoding that is expected by the downstream component.

cwes.name: Improper Enforcement of Behavioral Workflow
cwes.description: The product supports a session in which more than one behavior must be performed by an actor, but it does not properly ensure that the actor performs the behaviors in the required sequence.

cwes.name: Placement of User into Incorrect Group
cwes.description: The product or the administrator places a user into an incorrect group.

cwes.name: Missing Authorization
cwes.description: The product does not perform an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action.

cwes.name: Incorrect Authorization
cwes.description: The product performs an authorization check when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action, but it does not correctly perform the check.

cwes.name: Use of Uninitialized Resource
cwes.description: The product uses or accesses a resource that has not been initialized.

cwes.name: Missing Initialization of Resource
cwes.description: The product does not initialize a critical resource.

cwes.name: Use of Expired File Descriptor
cwes.description: The product uses or accesses a file descriptor after it has been closed.

cwes.name: Improper Update of Reference Count
cwes.description: The product uses a reference count to manage a resource, but it does not update or incorrectly updates the reference count.

cwes.name: Hidden Functionality
cwes.description: The product contains functionality that is not documented, not part of the specification, and not accessible through an interface or command sequence that is obvious to the product's users or administrators.

cwes.name: Improper Control of Dynamically-Managed Code Resources
cwes.description: The product does not properly restrict reading from or writing to dynamically-managed code resources such as variables, objects, classes, attributes, functions, or executable instructions or statements.

cwes.name: Improper Control of Dynamically-Identified Variables
cwes.description: The product does not properly restrict reading from or writing to dynamically-identified variables.

cwes.name: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
cwes.description: The product receives input from an upstream component that specifies multiple attributes, properties, or fields that are to be initialized or updated in an object, but it does not properly control which attributes can be modified.

cwes.name: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort
cwes.description: The product generates a hash for a password, but it uses a scheme that does not provide a sufficient level of computational effort that would make password cracking attacks infeasible or expensive.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an Expression Language Statement ('Expression Language Injection')
cwes.description: The product constructs all or part of an expression language (EL) statement in a framework such as a Java Server Page (JSP) using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended EL statement…

cwes.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
cwes.description: The web server receives a URL or similar request from an upstream component and retrieves the contents of this URL, but it does not sufficiently ensure that the request is being sent to the expected destination.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of Power Consumption
cwes.description: The product operates in an environment in which power is a limited resource that cannot be automatically replenished, but the product does not properly restrict the amount of power that its operation consumes.

cwes.name: Storage of Sensitive Data in a Mechanism without Access Control
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information in a file system or device that does not have built-in access control.

cwes.name: Insecure Storage of Sensitive Information
cwes.description: The product stores sensitive information without properly limiting read or write access by unauthorized actors.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of Communication Channel to Intended Endpoints
cwes.description: The product establishes a communication channel to (or from) an endpoint for privileged or protected operations, but it does not properly ensure that it is communicating with the correct endpoint.

cwes.name: Improper Enforcement of Message Integrity During Transmission in a Communication Channel
cwes.description: The product establishes a communication channel with an endpoint and receives a message from that endpoint, but it does not sufficiently ensure that the message was not modified during transmission.

cwes.name: Improper Verification of Intent by Broadcast Receiver
cwes.description: The Android application uses a Broadcast Receiver that receives an Intent but does not properly verify that the Intent came from an authorized source.

cwes.name: Improper Export of Android Application Components
cwes.description: The Android application exports a component for use by other applications, but does not properly restrict which applications can launch the component or access the data it contains.

cwes.name: Use of Implicit Intent for Sensitive Communication
cwes.description: The Android application uses an implicit intent for transmitting sensitive data to other applications.

cwes.name: Improper Verification of Source of a Communication Channel
cwes.description: The product establishes a communication channel to handle an incoming request that has been initiated by an actor, but it does not properly verify that the request is coming from the expected origin.

cwes.name: Incorrectly Specified Destination in a Communication Channel
cwes.description: The product creates a communication channel to initiate an outgoing request to an actor, but it does not correctly specify the intended destination for that actor.

cwes.name: Permissive Cross-domain Security Policy with Untrusted Domains
cwes.description:
The product uses a web-client protection
         mechanism such as a Content Security Policy (CSP) or
         cross-domain policy file, but the policy includes untrusted
         domains with which the web client is allowed to
         communicate.

cwes.name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic
cwes.description: The product generates a query intended to access or manipulate data in a data store such as a database, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that can modify the intended logic of the query.

cwes.name: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag
cwes.description: The product uses a cookie to store sensitive information, but the cookie is not marked with the HttpOnly flag.

cwes.name: Insufficient Visual Distinction of Homoglyphs Presented to User
cwes.description: The product displays information or identifiers to a user, but the display mechanism does not make it easy for the user to distinguish between visually similar or identical glyphs (homoglyphs), which may cause the user to misinterpret a glyph and perform an unintended, insecure action.

cwes.name: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames
cwes.description: The web application does not restrict or incorrectly restricts frame objects or UI layers that belong to another application or domain, which can lead to user confusion about which interface the user is interacting with.

cwes.name: Use of Web Link to Untrusted Target with window.opener Access
cwes.description: The web application produces links to untrusted external sites outside of its sphere of control, but it does not properly prevent the external site from modifying security-critical properties of the window.opener object, such as the location property.

cwes.name: Incomplete Comparison with Missing Factors
cwes.description: The product performs a comparison between entities that must consider multiple factors or characteristics of each entity, but the comparison does not include one or more of these factors.

cwes.name: Comparison of Incompatible Types
cwes.description: The product performs a comparison between two entities, but the entities are of different, incompatible types that cannot be guaranteed to provide correct results when they are directly compared.

cwes.name: Comparison Using Wrong Factors
cwes.description: The code performs a comparison between two entities, but the comparison examines the wrong factors or characteristics of the entities, which can lead to incorrect results and resultant weaknesses.

cwes.name: Processor Optimization Removal or Modification of Security-critical Code
cwes.description: The developer builds a security-critical protection mechanism into the software, but the processor optimizes the execution of the program such that the mechanism is removed or modified.

cwes.name: Insecure Automated Optimizations
cwes.description: The product uses a mechanism that automatically optimizes code, e.g. to improve a characteristic such as performance, but the optimizations can have an unintended side effect that might violate an intended security assumption.

cwes.name: Inadequate Detection or Handling of Adversarial Input Perturbations in Automated Recognition Mechanism
cwes.description: The product uses an automated mechanism such as machine learning to recognize complex data inputs (e.g. image or audio) as a particular concept or category, but it does not properly detect or handle inputs that have been modified or constructed in a way that causes the mechanism to detect a differen…

cwes.name: Use of Redundant Code
cwes.description:
The product has multiple functions, methods, procedures, macros, etc. that
					contain the same code.

cwes.name: Static Member Data Element outside of a Singleton Class Element
cwes.description:
The code contains a member element that is declared as static (but not final), in which
					its parent class element 
					is not a singleton class - that is, a class element that can be used only once in
					the 'to' association of a Create action.

cwes.name: Data Element Aggregating an Excessively Large Number of Non-Primitive Elements
cwes.description:
The product uses a data element that has an excessively large
					number of sub-elements with non-primitive data types such as structures or aggregated objects.

cwes.name: Architecture with Number of Horizontal Layers Outside of Expected Range
cwes.description:
The product's architecture contains too many - or too few -
					horizontal layers.

cwes.name: Parent Class with a Virtual Destructor and a Child Class without a Virtual Destructor
cwes.description: A parent class has a virtual destructor method, but the parent has a child class that does not have a virtual destructor.

cwes.name: Creation of Immutable Text Using String Concatenation
cwes.description: The product creates an immutable text string using string concatenation operations.

cwes.name: Modules with Circular Dependencies
cwes.description: The product contains modules in which one module has references that cycle back to itself, i.e., there are circular dependencies.

cwes.name: Invokable Control Element with Large Number of Outward Calls
cwes.description:
The code contains callable control elements that
         contain an excessively large number of references to other
         application objects external to the context of the callable,
         i.e. a Fan-Out value that is excessively large.

cwes.name: Excessive Data Query Operations in a Large Data Table
cwes.description:
The product performs a data query with a large number of joins
					and sub-queries on a large data table.

cwes.name: Excessive Platform Resource Consumption within a Loop
cwes.description:
The product has a loop body or loop condition that contains a control element that directly or
					indirectly consumes platform resources, e.g. messaging, sessions, locks, or file
					descriptors.

cwes.name: Initialization with Hard-Coded Network Resource Configuration Data
cwes.description: The product initializes data using hard-coded values that act as network resource identifiers.

cwes.name: Excessive Use of Hard-Coded Literals in Initialization
cwes.description:
The product initializes a data element using a hard-coded
					literal that is not a simple integer or static constant element.

cwes.name: Missing Documentation for Design
cwes.description: The product does not have documentation that represents how it is designed.

cwes.name: Invocation of a Control Element at an Unnecessarily Deep Horizontal Layer
cwes.description:
The code at one architectural layer invokes code that resides
					at a deeper layer than the adjacent layer, i.e., the invocation skips at least one
					layer, and the invoked code is not part of a vertical utility layer that can be referenced from any horizontal layer.

cwes.name: Multiple Inheritance from Concrete Classes
cwes.description:
The product contains a class with inheritance from more than
					one concrete class.

cwes.name: Invokable Control Element with Variadic Parameters
cwes.description:
A named-callable or method control element has a signature that
					supports a variable (variadic) number of parameters or arguments.

cwes.name: Data Access Operations Outside of Expected Data Manager Component
cwes.description: The product uses a dedicated, central data manager component as required by design, but it contains code that performs data-access operations that do not use this data manager.

cwes.name: Invokable Control Element in Multi-Thread Context with non-Final Static Storable or Member Element
cwes.description:
The code contains a function or method that
		 operates in a multi-threaded environment but owns an unsafe non-final
		                     static storable or member data element.

cwes.name: Insufficient Technical Documentation
cwes.description:
The product does not contain sufficient
         technical or engineering documentation (whether on paper or
         in electronic form) that contains descriptions of all the
         relevant software/hardware elements of the product, such as
         its usage, structure, architectural components…

cwes.name: Excessive Number of Inefficient Server-Side Data Accesses
cwes.description: The product performs too many data queries without using efficient data processing functionality such as stored procedures.

cwes.name: Insufficient Encapsulation
cwes.description: The product does not sufficiently hide the internal representation and implementation details of data or methods, which might allow external components or modules to modify data unexpectedly, invoke unexpected functionality, or introduce dependencies that the programmer did not intend.

cwes.name: Parent Class with References to Child Class
cwes.description: The code has a parent class that contains references to a child class, its methods, or its members.

cwes.name: Creation of Class Instance within a Static Code Block
